page: Vultr voc-g-96c-256gb-1280s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server powered by AMD EPYC-Rome processors. It features 96 vCPUs and 96 physical cores operating at 2.0 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The hardware configuration includes 256.0 GB of system memory, providing approximately 2.67 GB of RAM per core. For local storage, the instance is equipped with a 1280 GB NVMe SSD, and it includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

In benchmark testing, the server demonstrates strong single-core CPU performance with a stress-ng score of 2460.86, placing it in the top 25%. Its multi-core CPU performance is highly competitive, achieving a stress-ng score of 213580.47, which ranks in the top 10% tier. However, the CPU allocation is shared, which introduces potential performance variability under sustained, co-located tenant workloads. This shared resource model offers high core density and substantial memory capacity at a qualitative cost-efficiency advantage compared to dedicated instances, though it presents a tradeoff for workloads requiring guaranteed, consistent CPU performance.

Given its high core count and fast NVMe SSD storage, this server is suited for highly parallelized tasks such as multi-threaded batch processing, large-scale web serving, CI/CD pipelines, and distributed data processing. It can also support database workloads and development environments that benefit from high resource density and fast local storage, provided they can tolerate the shared CPU allocation model.
description: Vultr voc-g-96c-256gb-1280s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server featuring 96 AMD EPYC-Rome vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz, 256.0 GB of RAM, and a 1280 GB NVMe SSD. Operating on the x86_64 architecture, this instance delivers strong single-core performance with a stress-ng score of 2460.86 (top 25%) and top-tier multi-core performance with a stress-ng score of 213580.47 (top 10%). While the shared CPU allocation introduces potential performance variability, it provides high resource density and qualitative cost efficiency. This server is well-suited for highly parallelized workloads, including multi-threaded batch processing, large-scale web serving, CI/CD pipelines, and database environments that benefit from fast local NVMe storage.
